# CRITICAL: gh api graphql requires arrays to be passed as -f key[]=val1 -f key[]=val2
        # Using -f key=json.dumps([...]) passes the array as a literal string, not an array
        cmd = [
            "gh",
            "api",
            "graphql",
            "-f",
            f"query={GET_WORKFLOW_RUNS_BY_NODE_IDS_QUERY}",
        ]
        for node_id in node_ids:
            cmd.extend(["-f", f"nodeIds[]={node_id}"])
        stdout = execute_gh_command_with_retry(cmd, repo_root, self._time)
        response = json.loads(stdout)

        # Parse response into WorkflowRun objects
        return self._parse_workflow_runs_nodes_response(response, node_ids)

    def _parse_workflow_runs_nodes_response(
        self,
        response: dict[str, Any],
        node_ids: list[str],
    ) -> dict[str, WorkflowRun | None]:
        """Parse GraphQL nodes response into WorkflowRun objects.

        Maps the GraphQL checkSuite status/conclusion to WorkflowRun fields.

        Args:
            response: GraphQL response data
            node_ids: Original list of node IDs (for result ordering)

        Returns:
            Mapping of node_id -> WorkflowRun or None
        """
        result: dict[str, WorkflowRun | None] = {}
        nodes = response.get("data", {}).get("nodes", [])

        # Build mapping from id to parsed WorkflowRun
        for node in nodes:
            if node is None:
                continue

            node_id = node.get("id")
            if node_id is None:
                continue

            # Extract checkSuite data
            check_suite = node.get("checkSuite")
            status: WorkflowRunStatus | None = None
            conclusion: WorkflowRunConclusion | None = None
            head_sha: str | None = None

            if check_suite is not None:
                # Map GitHub checkSuite status to workflow run status
                cs_status = check_suite.get("status")
                cs_conclusion = check_suite.get("conclusion")

                # Map checkSuite.status to workflow run status
                if cs_status == "COMPLETED":
                    status = "completed"
                elif cs_status == "IN_PROGRESS":
                    status = "in_progress"
                elif cs_status == "QUEUED":
                    status = "queued"

                # Map checkSuite.conclusion to workflow run conclusion
                if cs_conclusion == "SUCCESS":
                    conclusion = "success"
                elif cs_conclusion == "FAILURE":
                    conclusion = "failure"
                elif cs_conclusion == "SKIPPED":
                    conclusion = "skipped"
                elif cs_conclusion == "CANCELLED":
                    conclusion = "cancelled"

                # Extract commit SHA
                commit = check_suite.get("commit")
                if commit is not None:
                    head_sha = commit.get("oid")

            # Parse created_at timestamp
            created_at = None
            created_at_str = node.get("createdAt")
            if created_at_str:
                created_at = datetime.fromisoformat(created_at_str.replace("Z", "+00:00"))

            workflow_run = WorkflowRun(
                run_id=str(node.get("databaseId", "")),
                status=status or "unknown",  # Default for missing status
                conclusion=conclusion,
                branch=BRANCH_NOT_AVAILABLE,
                head_sha=head_sha or "",  # Default for missing SHA
                display_title=DISPLAY_TITLE_NOT_AVAILABLE,
                created_at=created_at,
            )
            result[node_id] = workflow_run

        # Ensure all requested node_ids are in result (with None for missing)
        for node_id in node_ids:
            if node_id not in result:
                result[node_id] = None

        try:
            stdout = execute_gh_command_with_retry(cmd, location.root, self._time)
        except RuntimeError:
            return {}

        response = json.loads(stdout)
        repo_data = response.get("data", {}).get("repository", {})

        result: dict[int, dict[str, Any]] = {}
        for pr_num in pr_numbers:
            alias = f"pr_{pr_num}"
            node = repo_data.get(alias)
            if node is not None:
                result[pr_num] = node

        return result

    def _merge_rest_graphql_pr_data(
        self,
        rest_items: list[dict[str, Any]],
        enrichment: dict[int, dict[str, Any]],
        repo_id: GitHubRepoId,
    ) -> tuple[list[PRDetails], dict[int, list[PullRequestInfo]]]:
        """Merge REST issue data with GraphQL PR enrichment into PRDetails and PullRequestInfo.

        Args:
            rest_items: Raw REST API issue/PR items
            enrichment: GraphQL enrichment data keyed by PR number
            repo_id: GitHub repository identity

        Returns:
            Tuple of (pr_details_list, pr_linkages_by_pr_number)
        """
        pr_details_list: list[PRDetails] = []
        pr_linkages: dict[int, list[PullRequestInfo]] = {}

        for item in rest_items:
            pr_number = item["number"]
            gql = enrichment.get(pr_number, {})

            # Parse timestamps from REST
            created_at = datetime.fromisoformat(item["created_at"].replace("Z", "+00:00"))
            updated_at = datetime.fromisoformat(item["updated_at"].replace("Z", "+00:00"))

            # Parse labels from REST
            label_names = tuple(label["name"] for label in item.get("labels", []))

            # Author from REST
            pr_author = item.get("user", {}).get("login", "")

            # Draft and merge info from GraphQL enrichment
            is_draft = gql.get("isDraft", False)
            mergeable_raw = gql.get("mergeable", "UNKNOWN")
            merge_state_status = gql.get("mergeStateStatus", "UNKNOWN")
            is_cross_repository = gql.get("isCrossRepository", False)
            base_ref_name = gql.get("baseRefName", "")
            rest_head_ref = item.get("pull_request", {}).get("head", {}).get("ref", "")
            head_ref_name = gql.get("headRefName", rest_head_ref)

            # State from REST (normalize to upper)
            pr_state = item.get("state", "open").upper()

            pr_details = PRDetails(
                number=pr_number,
                url=item.get("html_url", ""),
                title=item.get("title", ""),
                body=item.get("body", "") or "",
                state=pr_state,
                is_draft=is_draft,
                base_ref_name=base_ref_name,
                head_ref_name=head_ref_name,
                is_cross_repository=is_cross_repository,
                mergeable=mergeable_raw,
                merge_state_status=merge_state_status,
                owner=repo_id.owner,
                repo=repo_id.repo,
                labels=label_names,
                created_at=created_at,
                updated_at=updated_at,
                author=pr_author,
            )
            pr_details_list.append(pr_details)

            # Build PullRequestInfo with rich GraphQL data
            checks_passing, checks_counts = self._parse_status_rollup(gql.get("statusCheckRollup"))
            has_conflicts = self._parse_mergeable_status(gql.get("mergeable"))
            review_thread_counts = self._parse_review_thread_counts(gql.get("reviewThreads"))
            review_decision = gql.get("reviewDecision")

            pr_info = PullRequestInfo(
                number=pr_number,
                state=pr_state,
                url=item.get("html_url", ""),
                is_draft=is_draft,
                title=item.get("title"),
                checks_passing=checks_passing,

    73→class RealPlanDataProvider(PlanDataProvider):
    74→    """Production implementation that wraps PlanListService.
    75→
    76→    Transforms PlanListData into PlanRowData for TUI display.
    77→    """
    78→
    79→    def __init__(
    80→        self,
    81→        ctx: ErkContext,
    82→        *,
    83→        location: GitHubRepoLocation,
    84→        clipboard: Clipboard,
    85→        browser: BrowserLauncher,
    86→        http_client: HttpClient,
    87→    ) -> None:
    88→        """Initialize with context and repository info.
    89→
    90→        Args:
    91→            ctx: ErkContext with all dependencies
    92→            location: GitHub repository location (local root + repo identity)
    93→            clipboard: Clipboard interface for copy operations
    94→            browser: BrowserLauncher interface for opening URLs
    95→            http_client: HTTP client for direct API calls (faster than subprocess)
    96→        """
    97→        self._ctx = ctx
    98→        self._location = location
    99→        self._clipboard = clipboard
   100→        self._browser = browser
   101→        self._http_client = http_client
   102→
   103→    @property
   104→    def repo_root(self) -> Path:
   105→        """Get the repository root path."""
   106→        return self._location.root
   107→
   108→    @property
   109→    def clipboard(self) -> Clipboard:
   110→        """Get the clipboard interface for copy operations."""
   111→        return self._clipboard
   112→
   113→    @property
   114→    def browser(self) -> BrowserLauncher:
   115→        """Get the browser launcher interface for opening URLs."""
   116→        return self._browser
   117→
   118→    def fetch_plans(self, filters: PlanFilters) -> tuple[list[PlanRowData], FetchTimings | None]:
   119→        """Fetch plans and transform to TUI row format.
   120→
   121→        Args:
   122→            filters: Filter options for the query
   123→
   124→        Returns:
   125→            Tuple of (list of PlanRowData for display, optional FetchTimings breakdown)
   126→        """
   127→        t_total_start = time.monotonic()
   128→
   129→        # Determine if we need workflow runs
   130→        needs_workflow_runs = filters.show_runs or filters.run_state is not None
   131→
   132→        # Route to the appropriate service based on the view's labels
   133→        if "erk-plan" in filters.labels:
   134→            plan_data = self._ctx.plan_list_service.get_plan_list_data(
   135→                location=self._location,
   136→                labels=list(filters.labels),
   137→                state=filters.state,
   138→                limit=filters.limit,
   139→                skip_workflow_runs=not needs_workflow_runs,
   140→                creator=filters.creator,
   141→                exclude_labels=list(filters.exclude_labels) if filters.exclude_labels else None,
   142→            )
   143→        else:
   144→            # Objectives use a dedicated service that always fetches via issues,
   145→            # regardless of the configured plan backend (draft PR vs issue)
   146→            plan_data = self._ctx.objective_list_service.get_objective_list_data(
   147→                location=self._location,
   148→                state=filters.state,
   149→                limit=filters.limit,
   150→                skip_workflow_runs=not needs_workflow_runs,
   151→                creator=filters.creator,
   152→            )
   153→
   154→        # Build local worktree mapping
   155→        t_wt_start = time.monotonic()
   156→        worktree_by_plan_id = self._build_worktree_mapping()
   157→        t_wt_end = time.monotonic()
   158→
   159→        # Use pre-converted Plan objects from PlanListData
   160→        plans = plan_data.plans
   161→
   162→        # Transform to PlanRowData
   163→        t_rows_start = time.monotonic()
   164→        rows: list[PlanRowData] = []
   165→        use_graphite = self._ctx.global_config.use_graphite if self._ctx.global_config else False
   166→
   167→        for plan in plans:
   168→            plan_id = int(plan.plan_identifier)
   169→
   170→            # Get workflow run for filtering
   171→            workflow_run = plan_data.workflow_runs.get(plan_id)
   172→
   173→            # Apply run_state filter
   174→            if filters.run_state is not None:
   175→                if workflow_run is None:
   176→                    continue
   177→                if get_workflow_run_state(workflow_run) != filters.run_state:
   178→                    continue
   179→
   180→            # Build row data
   181→            row = self._build_row_data(
   182→                plan=plan,
   183→                plan_id=plan_id,
   184→                pr_linkages=plan_data.pr_linkages,
   185→                workflow_run=workflow_run,
   186→                worktree_by_plan_id=worktree_by_plan_id,
   187→                use_graphite=use_graphite,
   188→            )
   189→            rows.append(row)
   190→        t_rows_end = time.monotonic()
   191→
   192→        # Build timing breakdown
   193→        # api_ms covers REST + GraphQL enrichment from PlanListData
   194→        # plan_parsing_ms covers body parsing from PlanListData
   195→        # workflow_runs_ms covers workflow run fetching from PlanListData
   196→        timings = FetchTimings(
   197→            rest_issues_ms=plan_data.api_ms,
   198→            graphql_enrich_ms=0.0,
   199→            plan_parsing_ms=plan_data.plan_parsing_ms,
   200→            workflow_runs_ms=plan_data.workflow_runs_ms,
   201→            worktree_mapping_ms=(t_wt_end - t_wt_start) * 1000,
   202→            row_building_ms=(t_rows_end - t_rows_start) * 1000,
   203→            total_ms=(t_rows_end - t_total_start) * 1000,
   204→        )
   205→
   206→        logger.info("fetch_plans timings: %s", timings.summary())
   207→
   208→        # Write timing to log file for post-execution analysis
   209→        self._append_timing_log(timings, len(rows))
   210→
   211→        return (rows, timings)
   212→
   213→    def close_plan(self, plan_id: int, plan_url: str) -> list[int]:
   214→        """Close a plan and its linked PRs using direct HTTP calls.
   215→
   216→        This method uses the HTTP client directly instead of subprocess calls
   217→        for significantly faster execution in the TUI.
   218→
   219→        Args:
   220→            plan_id: The plan ID to close
   221→            plan_url: The plan URL for PR linkage lookup
   222→
   223→        Returns:
   224→            List of PR numbers that were also closed
   225→        """
   226→        # Parse owner/repo from issue URL
   227→        owner_repo = self._parse_owner_repo_from_url(plan_url)
   228→        if owner_repo is None:
   229→            return []
   230→        owner, repo = owner_repo
   231→
   232→        # Close linked PRs first
   233→        closed_prs = self._close_linked_prs_http(plan_id, owner, repo)
   234→
   235→        # Close the plan (issue) via HTTP
   236→        self._http_client.patch(
   237→            f"repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{plan_id}",
   238→            data={"state": "closed"},
   239→        )
   240→
   241→        return closed_prs
   242→
   243→    def _parse_owner_repo_from_url(self, url: str) -> tuple[str, str] | None:
   244→        """Parse owner and repo from a GitHub URL.
   245→
   246→        Args:
   247→            url: GitHub URL (e.g., "https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123")
   248→
   249→        Returns:
   250→            Tuple of (owner, repo) or None if parsing fails
   251→        """
   252→        # URL format: https://github.com/owner/repo/...
   253→        if not url.startswith("https://github.com/"):
   254→            return None
   255→        parts = url.split("/")
   256→        # parts: ['https:', '', 'github.com', 'owner', 'repo', ...]
   257→        if len(parts) < 5:
   258→            return None
   259→        return (parts[3], parts[4])
   260→
   261→    def _close_linked_prs_http(
   262→        self,
   263→        plan_id: int,
   264→        owner: str,
   265→        repo: str,
   266→    ) -> list[int]:
   267→        """Close all OPEN PRs linked to an issue using HTTP.
   268→
   269→        Uses the GitHub REST API via HTTP client for fast execution.
   270→
   271→        Args:
   272→            plan_id: The plan ID
   273→            owner: Repository owner
   274→            repo: Repository name
   275→
   276→        Returns:
   277→            List of PR numbers that were closed
   278→        """
   279→        # Use the existing gateway to get PR linkages (still efficient for read)
   280→        location = GitHubRepoLocation(
   281→            root=self._location.root,
   282→            repo_id=GitHubRepoId(owner=owner, repo=repo),
   283→        )
   284→        pr_linkages = self._ctx.github.get_prs_linked_to_issues(location, [plan_id])
   285→        linked_prs = pr_linkages.get(plan_id, [])
   286→
   287→        closed_prs: list[int] = []
   288→        for pr in linked_prs:
   289→            if pr.state == "OPEN":
   290→                # Close via HTTP client for speed
   291→                self._http_client.patch(
   292→                    f"repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pr.number}",
   293→                    data={"state": "closed"},
   294→                )
   295→                closed_prs.append(pr.number)
   296→
   297→        return closed_prs
   298→
   299→    def submit_to_queue(self, plan_id: int, plan_url: str) -> None:
   300→        """Submit a plan to the implementation queue.
   301→
   302→        Runs 'erk pr dispatch' as a subprocess to handle the complex workflow
   303→        of creating branches, PRs, and triggering GitHub Actions.
   304→
   305→        Args:
   306→            plan_id: The plan ID to submit
   307→            plan_url: The plan URL (unused, kept for interface consistency)
   308→        """
   309→        # Run erk pr dispatch command from the repository root
   310→        # -f flag prevents blocking on existing branch prompts in TUI context
   311→        subprocess.run(
   312→            ["erk", "pr", "dispatch", str(plan_id), "-f"],
   313→            cwd=self._location.root,
   314→            check=True,
   315→            capture_output=True,
   316→        )
   317→
   318→    def fetch_branch_activity(self, rows: list[PlanRowData]) -> dict[int, BranchActivity]:
   319→        """Fetch branch activity for plans that exist locally.
   320→
   321→        Args:
   322→            rows: List of plan rows to fetch activity for
   323→
   324→        Returns:
   325→            Mapping of plan_id to BranchActivity for plans with local worktrees.
   326→        """
   327→        result: dict[int, BranchActivity] = {}
   328→
   329→        # Get trunk branch
   330→        trunk = self._ctx.git.branch.detect_trunk_branch(self._location.root)
   331→
   332→        for row in rows:
   333→            # Only fetch for plans with local branches
   334→            if not row.exists_locally or row.worktree_branch is None:
   335→                continue
   336→
   337→            # Get commits on branch not in trunk
   338→            commits = self._ctx.git.branch.get_branch_commits_with_authors(
   339→                self._location.root,
   340→                row.worktree_branch,
   341→                trunk,
   342→                limit=1,  # Only need most recent
   343→            )
   344→
   345→            if commits:
   346→                # Parse ISO timestamp from git
   347→                timestamp_str = commits[0]["timestamp"]
   348→                commit_at = datetime.fromisoformat(timestamp_str)
   349→                result[row.plan_id] = BranchActivity(
   350→                    last_commit_at=commit_at,
   351→                    last_commit_author=commits[0]["author"],
   352→                )
   353→            else:
   354→                result[row.plan_id] = BranchActivity.empty()
   355→
   356→        return result
   357→
   358→    def fetch_plan_content(self, plan_id: int, plan_body: str) -> str | None:
   359→        """Fetch plan content from the first comment of an issue, or return body directly.
   360→
   361→        For issue-based plans: uses plan_comment_id from the issue body metadata to
   362→        fetch the specific comment containing the plan content.
   363→
   364→        For draft PR plans: plan_body IS the plan content (already extracted from the
   365→        PR body by PlannedPRPlanListService). No metadata block is present, so return
   366→        plan_body directly.
   367→
   368→        Args:
   369→            plan_id: The GitHub issue/PR number
   370→            plan_body: The body text — either an issue body (with plan-header metadata
   371→                and plan_comment_id) or already-extracted plan content (draft PR case)
   372→
   373→        Returns:
   374→            The extracted plan content, or None if not found
   375→        """
   376→        # Check for plan-header metadata block to distinguish plan types:
   377→        # - Draft PR plans: no metadata block; plan_body IS the extracted content
   378→        # - Issue-based plans: metadata block with plan_comment_id pointing to the comment
   379→        block = find_metadata_block(plan_body, "plan-header")
   380→        if block is None:
   381→            return plan_body if plan_body.strip() else None
   382→
   383→        comment_id = block.data.get(PLAN_COMMENT_ID)
   384→        if not isinstance(comment_id, int):
   385→            return None
   386→
   387→        # Fetch the comment via HTTP client
   388→        owner = self._location.repo_id.owner
   389→        repo = self._location.repo_id.repo
   390→        endpoint = f"repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/comments/{comment_id}"
   391→
   392→        response = self._http_client.get(endpoint)
   393→        comment_body = response.get("body", "")
   394→
   395→        # Extract plan content from comment
   396→        return extract_plan_from_comment(comment_body)
   397→
   398→    def fetch_objective_content(self, plan_id: int, plan_body: str) -> str | None:
   399→        """Fetch objective content from the first comment of an issue.
   400→
   401→        Uses the objective_comment_id from the issue body metadata to fetch
   402→        the specific comment containing the objective content.
   403→
   404→        Args:
   405→            plan_id: The GitHub issue number
   406→            plan_body: The issue body (to extract objective_comment_id from metadata)
   407→
   408→        Returns:
   409→            The extracted objective content, or None if not found
   410→        """
   411→        comment_id = extract_objective_header_comment_id(plan_body)
   412→        if comment_id is None:
   413→            return None
   414→
   415→        owner = self._location.repo_id.owner
   416→        repo = self._location.repo_id.repo
   417→        endpoint = f"repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/comments/{comment_id}"
   418→
   419→        response = self._http_client.get(endpoint)
   420→        comment_body = response.get("body", "")
   421→
   422→        return extract_objective_from_comment(comment_body)
   423→
   424→    def fetch_plans_by_ids(self, plan_ids: set[int]) -> list[PlanRowData]:
   425→        """Fetch specific plans by their issue numbers.
   426→
   427→        Uses get_issues_by_numbers_with_pr_linkages for targeted fetching
   428→        instead of listing all plans and filtering.
   429→
   430→        Args:
   431→            plan_ids: Set of plan issue numbers to fetch
   432→
   433→        Returns:
   434→            List of PlanRowData objects sorted by plan_id
   435→        """
   436→        if not plan_ids:
   437→            return []
   438→
   439→        issues, pr_linkages = self._ctx.github.get_issues_by_numbers_with_pr_linkages(
   440→            location=self._location,
   441→            issue_numbers=list(plan_ids),
   442→        )
   443→
   444→        # Convert IssueInfo -> Plan
   445→        plans = [issue_info_to_plan(issue) for issue in issues]
   446→
   447→        # Build worktree mapping
   448→        worktree_by_plan_id = self._build_worktree_mapping()
   449→
   450→        # Build row data
   451→        use_graphite = self._ctx.global_config.use_graphite if self._ctx.global_config else False
   452→        rows: list[PlanRowData] = []
   453→        for plan in plans:
   454→            plan_id = int(plan.plan_identifier)
   455→            row = self._build_row_data(
   456→                plan=plan,
   457→                plan_id=plan_id,
   458→                pr_linkages=pr_linkages,
   459→                workflow_run=None,
   460→                worktree_by_plan_id=worktree_by_plan_id,
   461→                use_graphite=use_graphite,
   462→            )
   463→            rows.append(row)
   464→
   465→        rows.sort(key=lambda r: r.plan_id)
   466→        return rows
   467→
   468→    def fetch_plans_for_objective(self, objective_issue: int) -> list[PlanRowData]:
   469→        """Fetch plans associated with a specific objective.
   470→
   471→        Fetches all erk-plan issues (any state) and filters client-side by objective_issue.
   472→
   473→        Args:
   474→            objective_issue: The objective issue number to filter by
   475→
   476→        Returns:
   477→            List of PlanRowData objects for plans linked to this objective
   478→        """
   479→        filters = PlanFilters(
   480→            labels=("erk-plan",),
   481→            state=None,
   482→            run_state=None,
   483→            limit=100,
   484→            show_prs=True,
   485→            show_runs=False,
   486→            exclude_labels=(),
   487→            creator=None,
   488→        )
   489→        all_plans, _timings = self.fetch_plans(filters)
   490→        return [row for row in all_plans if row.objective_issue == objective_issue]
   491→
   492→    def fetch_unresolved_comments(self, pr_number: int) -> list[PRReviewThread]:
   493→        """Fetch unresolved review threads for a pull request.
   494→
   495→        Args:
   496→            pr_number: The PR number to fetch threads for
   497→
   498→        Returns:
   499→            List of unresolved PRReviewThread objects sorted by (path, line)
   500→        """
   501→        return self._ctx.github.get_pr_review_threads(
   502→            self._location.root, pr_number, include_resolved=False
   503→        )
   504→
   505→    def _append_timing_log(self, timings: FetchTimings, row_count: int) -> None:
   506→        """Append timing data to .erk/scratch/dash-timings.log for post-execution analysis.
   507→
   508→        Silently ignores all errors (sentinel paths in tests, missing directory,
   509→        permissions, etc.) since timing logs are strictly informational.
   510→
   511→        Args:
   512→            timings: Timing breakdown for this fetch cycle
   513→            row_count: Number of rows returned
   514→        """
   515→        try:
   516→            log_dir = self._location.root / ".erk" / "scratch"
   517→            if not log_dir.is_dir():
   518→                return
   519→            log_file = log_dir / "dash-timings.log"
   520→            timestamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
   521→            line = f"{timestamp}  rows={row_count}  {timings.summary()}\n"
   522→            with open(log_file, "a") as f:
   523→                f.write(line)
   524→        except Exception:
   525→            pass
   526→
   527→    def _build_worktree_mapping(self) -> dict[int, tuple[str, str | None]]:
   528→        """Build mapping of plan ID to (worktree name, branch).
   529→
   530→        Reads .impl/plan-ref.json from each worktree to discover the plan ID.
   531→        Plan-ref.json is the sole source of truth for plan-to-branch mapping.
   532→
   533→        Returns:
   534→            Mapping of plan ID to tuple of (worktree_name, branch_name)
   535→        """
   536→        _ensure_erk_metadata_dir_from_context(self._ctx.repo)
   537→        worktree_by_plan_id: dict[int, tuple[str, str | None]] = {}
   538→        worktrees = self._ctx.git.worktree.list_worktrees(self._location.root)
   539→        for worktree in worktrees:
   540→            impl_dir = worktree.path / ".impl"
   541→            plan_ref = read_plan_ref(impl_dir)
   542→            if plan_ref is not None and plan_ref.plan_id.isdigit():
   543→                issue_number = int(plan_ref.plan_id)
   544→                if issue_number not in worktree_by_plan_id:
   545→                    worktree_by_plan_id[issue_number] = (
   546→                        worktree.path.name,
   547→                        worktree.branch,
   548→                    )
   549→        return worktree_by_plan_id
   550→
   551→    def _build_row_data(
   552→        self,
   553→        *,
   554→        plan: Plan,
   555→        plan_id: int,
   556→        pr_linkages: dict[int, list[PullRequestInfo]],
   557→        workflow_run: WorkflowRun | None,
   558→        worktree_by_plan_id: dict[int, tuple[str, str | None]],
   559→        use_graphite: bool,
   560→    ) -> PlanRowData:
   561→        """Build a single PlanRowData from plan and related data."""
   562→        full_title = plan.title
   563→
   564→        # Worktree info
   565→        worktree_name = ""
   566→        worktree_branch: str | None = None
   567→        exists_locally = False
   568→
   569→        if plan_id in worktree_by_plan_id:
   570→            worktree_name, worktree_branch = worktree_by_plan_id[plan_id]
   571→            exists_locally = True
   572→
   573→        # Extract from pre-parsed header fields (no repeated YAML parsing)
   574→        local_impl_str: str | None = None
   575→        remote_impl_str: str | None = None
   576→        learn_status: str | None = None
   577→        learn_plan_issue: int | None = None
   578→        learn_plan_pr: int | None = None
   579→        learn_run_id: str | None = None
   580→        if plan.header_fields:
   581→            extracted = header_str(plan.header_fields, WORKTREE_NAME)
   582→            if extracted and not worktree_name:
   583→                worktree_name = extracted
   584→            local_impl_str = header_str(plan.header_fields, LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_AT)
   585→            remote_impl_str = header_str(plan.header_fields, LAST_REMOTE_IMPL_AT)
   586→            learn_status = header_str(plan.header_fields, LEARN_STATUS)
   587→            learn_plan_issue = header_int(plan.header_fields, LEARN_PLAN_ISSUE)
   588→            learn_plan_pr = header_int(plan.header_fields, LEARN_PLAN_PR)
   589→            learn_run_id = header_str(plan.header_fields, LEARN_RUN_ID)
   590→
   591→        # Extract objective_issue from pre-parsed header fields
   592→        objective_issue = header_int(plan.header_fields, OBJECTIVE_ISSUE)
   593→
   594→        # Learn plan issue closed state is not fetched (too slow for N sequential calls).
   595→        # Falls back to open icon (📋) which is acceptable.
   596→        learn_plan_issue_closed: bool | None = None
   597→
   598→        # Format learn display (full text for detail modal, icon-only for table)
   599→        learn_display = _format_learn_display(
   600→            learn_status,
   601→            learn_plan_issue,
   602→            learn_plan_pr,
   603→            learn_plan_issue_closed=learn_plan_issue_closed,
   604→        )
   605→        learn_display_icon = _format_learn_display_icon(
   606→            learn_status,
   607→            learn_plan_issue,
   608→            learn_plan_pr,
   609→            learn_plan_issue_closed=learn_plan_issue_closed,
   610→        )
   611→
   612→        # Get datetime versions for storage (already parsed by YAML)
   613→        last_local_impl_at = header_datetime(plan.header_fields, LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_AT)
   614→        last_remote_impl_at = header_datetime(plan.header_fields, LAST_REMOTE_IMPL_AT)
   615→
   616→        # Format time displays
   617→        local_impl = format_relative_time(local_impl_str)
   618→        local_impl_display = local_impl if local_impl else "-"
   619→        remote_impl = format_relative_time(remote_impl_str)
   620→        remote_impl_display = remote_impl if remote_impl else "-"
   621→
   622→        # PR info
   623→        pr_number: int | None = None
   624→        pr_url: str | None = None
   625→        pr_title: str | None = None
   626→        pr_state: str | None = None
   627→        pr_head_branch: str | None = None
   628→        pr_display = "-"
   629→        checks_display = "-"
   630→
   631→        # Comment counts - "-" when no PR
   632→        resolved_comment_count = 0
   633→        total_comment_count = 0
   634→        comments_display = "-"
   635→
   636→        # PR status fields for lifecycle enrichment
   637→        pr_is_draft: bool | None = None
   638→        pr_has_conflicts: bool | None = None
   639→        pr_review_decision: str | None = None
   640→        pr_checks_passing: bool | None = None
   641→        pr_has_unresolved_comments: bool | None = None
   642→        pr_is_stacked: bool | None = None
   643→
   644→        if plan_id in pr_linkages:
   645→            issue_prs = pr_linkages[plan_id]
   646→            selected_pr = select_display_pr(issue_prs, exclude_pr_numbers=None)
   647→            if selected_pr is not None:
   648→                pr_number = selected_pr.number
   649→                pr_title = selected_pr.title
   650→                pr_state = selected_pr.state
   651→                pr_head_branch = selected_pr.head_branch
   652→                graphite_url = self._ctx.graphite.get_graphite_url(
   653→                    GitHubRepoId(selected_pr.owner, selected_pr.repo), selected_pr.number
   654→                )
   655→                pr_url = graphite_url if use_graphite and graphite_url else selected_pr.url
   656→                emoji = get_pr_status_emoji(selected_pr)
   657→                if selected_pr.will_close_target:
   658→                    emoji += "🔗"
   659→                pr_display = f"#{selected_pr.number} {emoji}"
   660→                checks_display = format_checks_cell(selected_pr)
   661→
   662→                # Extract status fields for lifecycle enrichment
   663→                pr_is_draft = selected_pr.is_draft
   664→                pr_has_conflicts = selected_pr.has_conflicts
   665→                pr_review_decision = selected_pr.review_decision
   666→                pr_checks_passing = selected_pr.checks_passing
   667→                if selected_pr.base_ref_name is not None:
   668→                    pr_is_stacked = selected_pr.base_ref_name not in ("master", "main")
   669→
   670→                # Supplement with Graphite's local stack tracking — plan PRs
   671→                # always target master on GitHub even when Graphite-stacked
   672→                if pr_is_stacked is not True and pr_head_branch is not None:
   673→                    parent = self._ctx.branch_manager.get_parent_branch(
   674→                        self._location.root, pr_head_branch
   675→                    )
   676→                    if parent is not None and parent not in ("master", "main"):
   677→                        pr_is_stacked = True
   678→
   679→                # Get review thread counts from batched PR data
   680→                if selected_pr.review_thread_counts is not None:
   681→                    resolved_comment_count, total_comment_count = selected_pr.review_thread_counts
   682→                    comments_display = f"{resolved_comment_count}/{total_comment_count}"
   683→                    pr_has_unresolved_comments = total_comment_count > resolved_comment_count
   684→                else:
   685→                    comments_display = "0/0"
   686→                    pr_has_unresolved_comments = False
   687→
   688→        # Workflow run info
   689→        run_id: str | None = None
   690→        run_status: str | None = None
   691→        run_conclusion: str | None = None
   692→        run_id_display = "-"
   693→        run_state_display = "-"
   694→        run_url: str | None = None
   695→
   696→        if workflow_run is not None:
   697→            run_id = str(workflow_run.run_id)
   698→            run_status = workflow_run.status
   699→            run_conclusion = workflow_run.conclusion
   700→            if plan.url:
   701→                parts = plan.url.split("/")
   702→                if len(parts) >= 5:
   703→                    owner = parts[-4]
   704→                    repo_name = parts[-3]
   705→                    run_url = (
   706→                        f"https://github.com/{owner}/{repo_name}/actions/runs/{workflow_run.run_id}"
   707→                    )
   708→            run_id_display = format_workflow_run_id(workflow_run, run_url)
   709→            run_state_display = format_workflow_outcome(workflow_run)
   710→
   711→        # Log entries (empty for now - will be fetched on demand in the modal)
   712→        log_entries: tuple[tuple[str, str, str], ...] = ()
   713→
   714→        # Build learn_run_url for pending status
   715→        learn_run_url: str | None = None
   716→        if learn_run_id is not None and plan.url is not None:
   717→            parts = plan.url.split("/")
   718→            if len(parts) >= 5:
   719→                owner = parts[-4]
   720→                repo_name = parts[-3]
   721→                learn_run_url = (
   722→                    f"https://github.com/{owner}/{repo_name}/actions/runs/{learn_run_id}"
   723→                )
   724→
   725→        # Format objective display
   726→        objective_url = (
   727→            f"https://github.com/{self._location.repo_id.owner}/{self._location.repo_id.repo}/issues/{objective_issue}"
   728→            if objective_issue is not None
   729→            else None
   730→        )
   731→        objective_display = f"#{objective_issue}" if objective_issue is not None else "-"
   732→
   733→        # Compute slug display
   734→        objective_slug_display = "-"
   735→        if plan.body:
   736→            slug = extract_objective_slug(plan.body)
   737→            if slug is not None:
   738→                objective_slug_display = slug[:25]
   739→            elif plan.title:
   740→                stripped = plan.title.removeprefix("Objective: ")
   741→                objective_slug_display = stripped[:25]
   742→
   743→        # Parse roadmap for objective-specific fields
   744→        objective_done_nodes = 0
   745→        objective_total_nodes = 0
   746→        objective_progress_display = "-"
   747→        objective_state_display = "-"
   748→        objective_head_state = "-"
   749→        objective_next_node_display = "-"
   750→        objective_head_plans: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
   751→        if plan.body:
   752→            phases, _errors = parse_roadmap(plan.body)
   753→            if phases:
   754→                graph = build_graph(phases)
   755→                summary = compute_graph_summary(graph)
   756→                objective_done_nodes = summary["done"]
   757→                objective_total_nodes = summary["total_nodes"]
   758→                objective_progress_display = f"{objective_done_nodes}/{objective_total_nodes}"
   759→                objective_state_display = build_state_sparkline(graph.nodes)
   760→                next_node = find_graph_next_node(graph, phases)
   761→                if next_node is not None:
   762→                    objective_next_node_display = next_node["id"]
   763→                    node_status = next_node.get("status")
   764→                    min_status = graph.min_dep_status(next_node["id"])
   765→                    if node_status == "planning":
   766→                        objective_head_state = "planning"
   767→                    elif min_status is None or min_status in _TERMINAL_STATUSES:
   768→                        objective_head_state = "ready"
   769→                    else:
   770→                        objective_head_state = min_status.replace("_", " ")
   771→
   772→                    # Collect blocking dep PR numbers for the next node
   773→                    target = next((n for n in graph.nodes if n.id == next_node["id"]), None)
   774→                    if target is not None and target.depends_on:
   775→                        node_map = {n.id: n for n in graph.nodes}
   776→                        for dep_id in target.depends_on:
   777→                            if dep_id in node_map:
   778→                                dep = node_map[dep_id]
   779→                                if dep.status not in _TERMINAL_STATUSES and dep.pr is not None:
   780→                                    num = dep.pr.lstrip("#")
   781→                                    repo_id = self._location.repo_id
   782→                                    url = f"https://github.com/{repo_id.owner}/{repo_id.repo}/issues/{num}"
   783→                                    objective_head_plans.append((dep.pr, url))
   784→
   785→        # Format updated_at display
   786→        updated_display = format_relative_time(plan.updated_at.isoformat()) or "-"
   787→
   788→        # Format created_at display
   789→        created_display = format_relative_time(plan.created_at.isoformat()) or "-"
   790→
   791→        # Determine if this is a learn plan
   792→        is_learn_plan = "erk-learn" in plan.labels
   793→
   794→        # Compute lifecycle display from header or infer from metadata
   795→        lifecycle_display = _compute_lifecycle_display(
   796→            plan, has_workflow_run=workflow_run is not None
   797→        )
   798→
   799→        # Compute status indicators separately for the "sts" column
   800→        status_display = compute_status_indicators(
   801→            lifecycle_display,
   802→            is_draft=pr_is_draft,
   803→            has_conflicts=pr_has_conflicts,
   804→            review_decision=pr_review_decision,
   805→            checks_passing=pr_checks_passing,
   806→            has_unresolved_comments=pr_has_unresolved_comments,
   807→            is_stacked=pr_is_stacked,
   808→        )
   809→
   810→        return PlanRowData(
   811→            plan_id=plan_id,
   812→            plan_url=plan.url,
   813→            pr_number=pr_number,
   814→            pr_url=pr_url,
   815→            pr_display=pr_display,
   816→            checks_display=checks_display,
   817→            worktree_name=worktree_name,
   818→            exists_locally=exists_locally,
   819→            local_impl_display=local_impl_display,
   820→            remote_impl_display=remote_impl_display,
   821→            run_id_display=run_id_display,
   822→            run_state_display=run_state_display,
   823→            run_url=run_url,
   824→            full_title=full_title,
   825→            plan_body=plan.body or "",
   826→            pr_title=pr_title,
   827→            pr_state=pr_state,
   828→            pr_head_branch=pr_head_branch,
   829→            worktree_branch=worktree_branch,
   830→            last_local_impl_at=last_local_impl_at,
   831→            last_remote_impl_at=last_remote_impl_at,
   832→            run_id=run_id,
   833→            run_status=run_status,
   834→            run_conclusion=run_conclusion,
   835→            log_entries=log_entries,
   836→            resolved_comment_count=resolved_comment_count,
   837→            total_comment_count=total_comment_count,
   838→            comments_display=comments_display,
   839→            learn_status=learn_status,
   840→            learn_plan_issue=learn_plan_issue,
   841→            learn_plan_issue_closed=learn_plan_issue_closed,
   842→            learn_plan_pr=learn_plan_pr,
   843→            learn_run_url=learn_run_url,
   844→            learn_display=learn_display,
   845→            learn_display_icon=learn_display_icon,
   846→            objective_issue=objective_issue,
   847→            objective_url=objective_url,
   848→            objective_display=objective_display,
   849→            objective_done_nodes=objective_done_nodes,
   850→            objective_total_nodes=objective_total_nodes,
   851→            objective_progress_display=objective_progress_display,
   852→            objective_slug_display=objective_slug_display,
   853→            objective_state_display=objective_state_display,
   854→            objective_head_state=objective_head_state,
   855→            objective_head_plans=tuple(objective_head_plans),
   856→            objective_next_node_display=objective_next_node_display,
   857→            updated_at=plan.updated_at,
   858→            updated_display=updated_display,
   859→            created_at=plan.created_at,
   860→            created_display=created_display,
   861→            author=str(plan.metadata.get("author", "")),
   862→            is_learn_plan=is_learn_plan,
   863→            lifecycle_display=lifecycle_display,
   864→            status_display=status_display,
   865→        )
   866→
   867→
   868→def _format_learn_display(
   869→    learn_status: str | None,
   870→    learn_plan_issue: int | None,
   871→    learn_plan_pr: int | None,
   872→    *,
   873→    learn_plan_issue_closed: bool | None,
   874→) -> str:
   875→    """Format learn status for display with inline descriptions.
   876→
   877→    Args:
   878→        learn_status: Raw status value from plan header
   879→        learn_plan_issue: Issue number of generated learn plan
   880→        learn_plan_pr: PR number that implemented the learn plan
   881→        learn_plan_issue_closed: Whether the learn plan issue is closed
   882→
   883→    Returns:
   884→        Formatted display string based on status:
   885→        - None or "not_started" -> "- not started"
   886→        - "pending" -> "⟳ in progress"
   887→        - "completed_no_plan" -> "∅ no insights"
   888→        - "completed_with_plan" + closed -> "✅ #456" (using learn_plan_issue)
   889→        - "completed_with_plan" + open -> "📋 #456" (using learn_plan_issue)
   890→        - "pending_review" -> "🚧 #789" (using learn_plan_pr for draft PR)
   891→        - "plan_completed" -> "✓ #12" (using learn_plan_pr)
   892→    """
   893→    if learn_status is None or learn_status == "not_started":
   894→        return "- not started"
   895→    if learn_status == "pending":
   896→        return "⟳ in progress"
   897→    if learn_status == "completed_no_plan":
   898→        return "∅ no insights"
   899→    if learn_status == "completed_with_plan" and learn_plan_issue is not None:
   900→        if learn_plan_issue_closed is True:
   901→            return f"✅ #{learn_plan_issue}"
   902→        return f"📋 #{learn_plan_issue}"
   903→    if learn_status == "pending_review" and learn_plan_pr is not None:
   904→        return f"🚧 #{learn_plan_pr}"
   905→    if learn_status == "plan_completed" and learn_plan_pr is not None:
   906→        return f"✓ #{learn_plan_pr}"
   907→    # Fallback for unknown status
   908→    return "- not started"
   909→
   910→
   911→def _format_learn_display_icon(
   912→    learn_status: str | None,
   913→    learn_plan_issue: int | None,
   914→    learn_plan_pr: int | None,
   915→    *,
   916→    learn_plan_issue_closed: bool | None,
   917→) -> str:
   918→    """Format learn status as icon-only for table display.
   919→
   920→    Args:
   921→        learn_status: Raw status value from plan header
   922→        learn_plan_issue: Issue number of generated learn plan
   923→        learn_plan_pr: PR number that implemented the learn plan
   924→        learn_plan_issue_closed: Whether the learn plan issue is closed
   925→
   926→    Returns:
   927→        Icon-only display string based on status:
   928→        - None or "not_started" -> "-"
   929→        - "pending" -> "⟳"
   930→        - "completed_no_plan" -> "∅"
   931→        - "completed_with_plan" + closed -> "✅ #456" (using learn_plan_issue)
   932→        - "completed_with_plan" + open -> "📋 #456" (using learn_plan_issue)
   933→        - "pending_review" -> "🚧 #789" (using learn_plan_pr for draft PR)
   934→        - "plan_completed" -> "✓ #12" (using learn_plan_pr)
   935→    """
   936→    if learn_status is None or learn_status == "not_started":
   937→        return "-"
   938→    if learn_status == "pending":
   939→        return "⟳"
   940→    if learn_status == "completed_no_plan":
   941→        return "∅"
   942→    if learn_status == "completed_with_plan" and learn_plan_issue is not None:
   943→        if learn_plan_issue_closed is True:
   944→            return f"✅ #{learn_plan_issue}"
   945→        return f"📋 #{learn_plan_issue}"
   946→    if learn_status == "pending_review" and learn_plan_pr is not None:
   947→        return f"🚧 #{learn_plan_pr}"
   948→    if learn_status == "plan_completed" and learn_plan_pr is not None:
   949→        return f"✓ #{learn_plan_pr}"
   950→    # Fallback for unknown status
   951→    return "-"
   952→
   953→
   954→def _compute_lifecycle_display(plan: Plan, *, has_workflow_run: bool) -> str:
   955→    """Compute lifecycle stage display string for a plan.
   956→
   957→    Delegates to lifecycle.compute_lifecycle_display. This wrapper preserves
   958→    the module-private name used by callers within this file.
   959→    """
   960→    from erk_shared.gateway.plan_data_provider.lifecycle import compute_lifecycle_display
   961→
   962→    return compute_lifecycle_display(plan, has_workflow_run=has_workflow_run)
   963→
   964→
   965→def _ensure_erk_metadata_dir_from_context(repo: RepoContext | NoRepoSentinel) -> None:
   966→    """Ensure erk metadata directory exists, handling sentinel case."""
   967→    if isinstance(repo, RepoContext):
   968→        ensure_erk_metadata_dir(repo)
   969→

file.content:
"""Real implementation of PlanDataProvider for production use."""

import logging
import subprocess
import time
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path

from erk.core.context import ErkContext
from erk.core.display_utils import (
    format_relative_time,
    format_workflow_outcome,
    format_workflow_run_id,
    get_workflow_run_state,
)
from erk.core.pr_utils import select_display_pr
from erk.core.repo_discovery import NoRepoSentinel, RepoContext, ensure_erk_metadata_dir
from erk.tui.data.types import FetchTimings, PlanFilters, PlanRowData
from erk.tui.sorting.types import BranchActivity
from erk_shared.gateway.browser.abc import BrowserLauncher
from erk_shared.gateway.clipboard.abc import Clipboard
from erk_shared.gateway.github.emoji import format_checks_cell, get_pr_status_emoji
from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import (
    extract_objective_from_comment,
    extract_objective_header_comment_id,
    extract_objective_slug,
    find_metadata_block,
)
from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.dependency_graph import (
    _TERMINAL_STATUSES,
    build_graph,
    build_state_sparkline,
    compute_graph_summary,
    find_graph_next_node,
)
from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.plan_header import extract_plan_from_comment
from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.roadmap import (
    parse_roadmap,
)
from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.schemas import (
    LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_AT,
    LAST_REMOTE_IMPL_AT,
    LEARN_PLAN_ISSUE,
    LEARN_PLAN_PR,
    LEARN_RUN_ID,
    LEARN_STATUS,
    OBJECTIVE_ISSUE,
    PLAN_COMMENT_ID,
    WORKTREE_NAME,
)
from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import (
    GitHubRepoId,
    GitHubRepoLocation,
    PRReviewThread,
    PullRequestInfo,
    WorkflowRun,
)
from erk_shared.gateway.http.abc import HttpClient
from erk_shared.gateway.plan_data_provider.abc import PlanDataProvider
from erk_shared.gateway.plan_data_provider.lifecycle import compute_status_indicators
from erk_shared.impl_folder import read_plan_ref
from erk_shared.plan_store.conversion import (
    header_datetime,
    header_int,
    header_str,
    issue_info_to_plan,
)
from erk_shared.plan_store.types import Plan

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class RealPlanDataProvider(PlanDataProvider):
    """Production implementation that wraps PlanListService.

    Transforms PlanListData into PlanRowData for TUI display.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        ctx: ErkContext,
        *,
        location: GitHubRepoLocation,
        clipboard: Clipboard,
        browser: BrowserLauncher,
        http_client: HttpClient,
    ) -> None:
        """Initialize with context and repository info.

        Args:
            ctx: ErkContext with all dependencies
            location: GitHub repository location (local root + repo identity)
            clipboard: Clipboard interface for copy operations
            browser: BrowserLauncher interface for opening URLs
            http_client: HTTP client for direct API calls (faster than subprocess)
        """
        self._ctx = ctx
        self._location = location
        self._clipboard = clipboard
        self._browser = browser
        self._http_client = http_client

    @property
    def repo_root(self) -> Path:
        """Get the repository root path."""
        return self._location.root

    @property
    def clipboard(self) -> Clipboard:
        """Get the clipboard interface for copy operations."""
        return self._clipboard

    @property
    def browser(self) -> BrowserLauncher:
        """Get the browser launcher interface for opening URLs."""
        return self._browser

    def fetch_plans(self, filters: PlanFilters) -> tuple[list[PlanRowData], FetchTimings | None]:
        """Fetch plans and transform to TUI row format.

        Args:
            filters: Filter options for the query

        Returns:
            Tuple of (list of PlanRowData for display, optional FetchTimings breakdown)
        """
        t_total_start = time.monotonic()

        # Determine if we need workflow runs
        needs_workflow_runs = filters.show_runs or filters.run_state is not None

        # Route to the appropriate service based on the view's labels
        if "erk-plan" in filters.labels:
            plan_data = self._ctx.plan_list_service.get_plan_list_data(
                location=self._location,
                labels=list(filters.labels),
                state=filters.state,
                limit=filters.limit,
                skip_workflow_runs=not needs_workflow_runs,
                creator=filters.creator,
                exclude_labels=list(filters.exclude_labels) if filters.exclude_labels else None,
            )
        else:
            # Objectives use a dedicated service that always fetches via issues,
            # regardless of the configured plan backend (draft PR vs issue)
            plan_data = self._ctx.objective_list_service.get_objective_list_data(
                location=self._location,
                state=filters.state,
                limit=filters.limit,
                skip_workflow_runs=not needs_workflow_runs,
                creator=filters.creator,
            )

        # Build local worktree mapping
        t_wt_start = time.monotonic()
        worktree_by_plan_id = self._build_worktree_mapping()
        t_wt_end = time.monotonic()

        # Use pre-converted Plan objects from PlanListData
        plans = plan_data.plans

        # Transform to PlanRowData
        t_rows_start = time.monotonic()
        rows: list[PlanRowData] = []
        use_graphite = self._ctx.global_config.use_graphite if self._ctx.global_config else False

        for plan in plans:
            plan_id = int(plan.plan_identifier)

            # Get workflow run for filtering
            workflow_run = plan_data.workflow_runs.get(plan_id)

            # Apply run_state filter
            if filters.run_state is not None:
                if workflow_run is None:
                    continue
                if get_workflow_run_state(workflow_run) != filters.run_state:
                    continue

            # Build row data
            row = self._build_row_data(
                plan=plan,
                plan_id=plan_id,
                pr_linkages=plan_data.pr_linkages,
                workflow_run=workflow_run,
                worktree_by_plan_id=worktree_by_plan_id,
                use_graphite=use_graphite,
            )
            rows.append(row)
        t_rows_end = time.monotonic()

        # Build timing breakdown
        # api_ms covers REST + GraphQL enrichment from PlanListData
        # plan_parsing_ms covers body parsing from PlanListData
        # workflow_runs_ms covers workflow run fetching from PlanListData
        timings = FetchTimings(
            rest_issues_ms=plan_data.api_ms,
            graphql_enrich_ms=0.0,
            plan_parsing_ms=plan_data.plan_parsing_ms,
            workflow_runs_ms=plan_data.workflow_runs_ms,
            worktree_mapping_ms=(t_wt_end - t_wt_start) * 1000,
            row_building_ms=(t_rows_end - t_rows_start) * 1000,
            total_ms=(t_rows_end - t_total_start) * 1000,
        )

        logger.info("fetch_plans timings: %s", timings.summary())

        # Write timing to log file for post-execution analysis
        self._append_timing_log(timings, len(rows))

        return (rows, timings)

    def close_plan(self, plan_id: int, plan_url: str) -> list[int]:
        """Close a plan and its linked PRs using direct HTTP calls.

        This method uses the HTTP client directly instead of subprocess calls
        for significantly faster execution in the TUI.

        Args:
            plan_id: The plan ID to close
            plan_url: The plan URL for PR linkage lookup

        Returns:
            List of PR numbers that were also closed
        """
        # Parse owner/repo from issue URL
        owner_repo = self._parse_owner_repo_from_url(plan_url)
        if owner_repo is None:
            return []
        owner, repo = owner_repo

        # Close linked PRs first
        closed_prs = self._close_linked_prs_http(plan_id, owner, repo)

        # Close the plan (issue) via HTTP
        self._http_client.patch(
            f"repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{plan_id}",
            data={"state": "closed"},
        )

        return closed_prs

    def _parse_owner_repo_from_url(self, url: str) -> tuple[str, str] | None:
        """Parse owner and repo from a GitHub URL.

        Args:
            url: GitHub URL (e.g., "https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123")

        Returns:
            Tuple of (owner, repo) or None if parsing fails
        """
        # URL format: https://github.com/owner/repo/...
        if not url.startswith("https://github.com/"):
            return None
        parts = url.split("/")
        # parts: ['https:', '', 'github.com', 'owner', 'repo', ...]
        if len(parts) < 5:
            return None
        return (parts[3], parts[4])

    def _close_linked_prs_http(
        self,
        plan_id: int,
        owner: str,
        repo: str,
    ) -> list[int]:
        """Close all OPEN PRs linked to an issue using HTTP.

        Uses the GitHub REST API via HTTP client for fast execution.

        Args:
            plan_id: The plan ID
            owner: Repository owner
            repo: Repository name

        Returns:
            List of PR numbers that were closed
        """
        # Use the existing gateway to get PR linkages (still efficient for read)
        location = GitHubRepoLocation(
            root=self._location.root,
            repo_id=GitHubRepoId(owner=owner, repo=repo),
        )
        pr_linkages = self._ctx.github.get_prs_linked_to_issues(location, [plan_id])
        linked_prs = pr_linkages.get(plan_id, [])

        closed_prs: list[int] = []
        for pr in linked_prs:
            if pr.state == "OPEN":
                # Close via HTTP client for speed
                self._http_client.patch(
                    f"repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pr.number}",
                    data={"state": "closed"},
                )
                closed_prs.append(pr.number)

        return closed_prs

    def submit_to_queue(self, plan_id: int, plan_url: str) -> None:
        """Submit a plan to the implementation queue.

        Runs 'erk pr dispatch' as a subprocess to handle the complex workflow
        of creating branches, PRs, and triggering GitHub Actions.

        Args:
            plan_id: The plan ID to submit
            plan_url: The plan URL (unused, kept for interface consistency)
        """
        # Run erk pr dispatch command from the repository root
        # -f flag prevents blocking on existing branch prompts in TUI context
        subprocess.run(
            ["erk", "pr", "dispatch", str(plan_id), "-f"],
            cwd=self._location.root,
            check=True,
            capture_output=True,
        )

    def fetch_branch_activity(self, rows: list[PlanRowData]) -> dict[int, BranchActivity]:
        """Fetch branch activity for plans that exist locally.

        Args:
            rows: List of plan rows to fetch activity for

        Returns:
            Mapping of plan_id to BranchActivity for plans with local worktrees.
        """
        result: dict[int, BranchActivity] = {}

        # Get trunk branch
        trunk = self._ctx.git.branch.detect_trunk_branch(self._location.root)

        for row in rows:
            # Only fetch for plans with local branches
            if not row.exists_locally or row.worktree_branch is None:
                continue

            # Get commits on branch not in trunk
            commits = self._ctx.git.branch.get_branch_commits_with_authors(
                self._location.root,
                row.worktree_branch,
                trunk,
                limit=1,  # Only need most recent
            )

            if commits:
                # Parse ISO timestamp from git
                timestamp_str = commits[0]["timestamp"]
                commit_at = datetime.fromisoformat(timestamp_str)
                result[row.plan_id] = BranchActivity(
                    last_commit_at=commit_at,
                    last_commit_author=commits[0]["author"],
                )
            else:
                result[row.plan_id] = BranchActivity.empty()

        return result

    def fetch_plan_content(self, plan_id: int, plan_body: str) -> str | None:
        """Fetch plan content from the first comment of an issue, or return body directly.

        For issue-based plans: uses plan_comment_id from the issue body metadata to
        fetch the specific comment containing the plan content.

        For draft PR plans: plan_body IS the plan content (already extracted from the
        PR body by PlannedPRPlanListService). No metadata block is present, so return
        plan_body directly.

        Args:
            plan_id: The GitHub issue/PR number
            plan_body: The body text — either an issue body (with plan-header metadata
                and plan_comment_id) or already-extracted plan content (draft PR case)

        Returns:
            The extracted plan content, or None if not found
        """
        # Check for plan-header metadata block to distinguish plan types:
        # - Draft PR plans: no metadata block; plan_body IS the extracted content
        # - Issue-based plans: metadata block with plan_comment_id pointing to the comment
        block = find_metadata_block(plan_body, "plan-header")
        if block is None:
            return plan_body if plan_body.strip() else None

        comment_id = block.data.get(PLAN_COMMENT_ID)
        if not isinstance(comment_id, int):
            return None

        # Fetch the comment via HTTP client
        owner = self._location.repo_id.owner
        repo = self._location.repo_id.repo
        endpoint = f"repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/comments/{comment_id}"

        response = self._http_client.get(endpoint)
        comment_body = response.get("body", "")

        # Extract plan content from comment
        return extract_plan_from_comment(comment_body)

    def fetch_objective_content(self, plan_id: int, plan_body: str) -> str | None:
        """Fetch objective content from the first comment of an issue.

        Uses the objective_comment_id from the issue body metadata to fetch
        the specific comment containing the objective content.

        Args:
            plan_id: The GitHub issue number
            plan_body: The issue body (to extract objective_comment_id from metadata)

        Returns:
            The extracted objective content, or None if not found
        """
        comment_id = extract_objective_header_comment_id(plan_body)
        if comment_id is None:
            return None

        owner = self._location.repo_id.owner
        repo = self._location.repo_id.repo
        endpoint = f"repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/comments/{comment_id}"

        response = self._http_client.get(endpoint)
        comment_body = response.get("body", "")

        return extract_objective_from_comment(comment_body)

    def fetch_plans_by_ids(self, plan_ids: set[int]) -> list[PlanRowData]:
        """Fetch specific plans by their issue numbers.

        Uses get_issues_by_numbers_with_pr_linkages for targeted fetching
        instead of listing all plans and filtering.

        Args:
            plan_ids: Set of plan issue numbers to fetch

        Returns:
            List of PlanRowData objects sorted by plan_id
        """
        if not plan_ids:
            return []

        issues, pr_linkages = self._ctx.github.get_issues_by_numbers_with_pr_linkages(
            location=self._location,
            issue_numbers=list(plan_ids),
        )

        # Convert IssueInfo -> Plan
        plans = [issue_info_to_plan(issue) for issue in issues]

        # Build worktree mapping
        worktree_by_plan_id = self._build_worktree_mapping()

        # Build row data
        use_graphite = self._ctx.global_config.use_graphite if self._ctx.global_config else False
        rows: list[PlanRowData] = []
        for plan in plans:
            plan_id = int(plan.plan_identifier)
            row = self._build_row_data(
                plan=plan,
                plan_id=plan_id,
                pr_linkages=pr_linkages,
                workflow_run=None,
                worktree_by_plan_id=worktree_by_plan_id,
                use_graphite=use_graphite,
            )
            rows.append(row)

        rows.sort(key=lambda r: r.plan_id)
        return rows

    def fetch_plans_for_objective(self, objective_issue: int) -> list[PlanRowData]:
        """Fetch plans associated with a specific objective.

        Fetches all erk-plan issues (any state) and filters client-side by objective_issue.

        Args:
            objective_issue: The objective issue number to filter by

        Returns:
            List of PlanRowData objects for plans linked to this objective
        """
        filters = PlanFilters(
            labels=("erk-plan",),
            state=None,
            run_state=None,
            limit=100,
            show_prs=True,
            show_runs=False,
            exclude_labels=(),
            creator=None,
        )
        all_plans, _timings = self.fetch_plans(filters)
        return [row for row in all_plans if row.objective_issue == objective_issue]

    def fetch_unresolved_comments(self, pr_number: int) -> list[PRReviewThread]:
        """Fetch unresolved review threads for a pull request.

        Args:
            pr_number: The PR number to fetch threads for

        Returns:
            List of unresolved PRReviewThread objects sorted by (path, line)
        """
        return self._ctx.github.get_pr_review_threads(
            self._location.root, pr_number, include_resolved=False
        )

    def _append_timing_log(self, timings: FetchTimings, row_count: int) -> None:
        """Append timing data to .erk/scratch/dash-timings.log for post-execution analysis.

        Silently ignores all errors (sentinel paths in tests, missing directory,
        permissions, etc.) since timing logs are strictly informational.

        Args:
            timings: Timing breakdown for this fetch cycle
            row_count: Number of rows returned
        """
        try:
            log_dir = self._location.root / ".erk" / "scratch"
            if not log_dir.is_dir():
                return
            log_file = log_dir / "dash-timings.log"
            timestamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
            line = f"{timestamp}  rows={row_count}  {timings.summary()}\n"
            with open(log_file, "a") as f:
                f.write(line)
        except Exception:
            pass

    def _build_worktree_mapping(self) -> dict[int, tuple[str, str | None]]:
        """Build mapping of plan ID to (worktree name, branch).

        Reads .impl/plan-ref.json from each worktree to discover the plan ID.
        Plan-ref.json is the sole source of truth for plan-to-branch mapping.

        Returns:
            Mapping of plan ID to tuple of (worktree_name, branch_name)
        """
        _ensure_erk_metadata_dir_from_context(self._ctx.repo)
        worktree_by_plan_id: dict[int, tuple[str, str | None]] = {}
        worktrees = self._ctx.git.worktree.list_worktrees(self._location.root)
        for worktree in worktrees:
            impl_dir = worktree.path / ".impl"
            plan_ref = read_plan_ref(impl_dir)
            if plan_ref is not None and plan_ref.plan_id.isdigit():
                issue_number = int(plan_ref.plan_id)
                if issue_number not in worktree_by_plan_id:
                    worktree_by_plan_id[issue_number] = (
                        worktree.path.name,
                        worktree.branch,
                    )
        return worktree_by_plan_id

    def _build_row_data(
        self,
        *,
        plan: Plan,
        plan_id: int,
        pr_linkages: dict[int, list[PullRequestInfo]],
        workflow_run: WorkflowRun | None,
        worktree_by_plan_id: dict[int, tuple[str, str | None]],
        use_graphite: bool,
    ) -> PlanRowData:
        """Build a single PlanRowData from plan and related data."""
        full_title = plan.title

        # Worktree info
        worktree_name = ""
        worktree_branch: str | None = None
        exists_locally = False

        if plan_id in worktree_by_plan_id:
            worktree_name, worktree_branch = worktree_by_plan_id[plan_id]
            exists_locally = True

        # Extract from pre-parsed header fields (no repeated YAML parsing)
        local_impl_str: str | None = None
        remote_impl_str: str | None = None
        learn_status: str | None = None
        learn_plan_issue: int | None = None
        learn_plan_pr: int | None = None
        learn_run_id: str | None = None
        if plan.header_fields:
            extracted = header_str(plan.header_fields, WORKTREE_NAME)
            if extracted and not worktree_name:
                worktree_name = extracted
            local_impl_str = header_str(plan.header_fields, LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_AT)
            remote_impl_str = header_str(plan.header_fields, LAST_REMOTE_IMPL_AT)
            learn_status = header_str(plan.header_fields, LEARN_STATUS)
            learn_plan_issue = header_int(plan.header_fields, LEARN_PLAN_ISSUE)
            learn_plan_pr = header_int(plan.header_fields, LEARN_PLAN_PR)
            learn_run_id = header_str(plan.header_fields, LEARN_RUN_ID)

        # Extract objective_issue from pre-parsed header fields
        objective_issue = header_int(plan.header_fields, OBJECTIVE_ISSUE)

        # Learn plan issue closed state is not fetched (too slow for N sequential calls).
        # Falls back to open icon (📋) which is acceptable.
        learn_plan_issue_closed: bool | None = None

        # Format learn display (full text for detail modal, icon-only for table)
        learn_display = _format_learn_display(
            learn_status,
            learn_plan_issue,
            learn_plan_pr,
            learn_plan_issue_closed=learn_plan_issue_closed,
        )
        learn_display_icon = _format_learn_display_icon(
            learn_status,
            learn_plan_issue,
            learn_plan_pr,
            learn_plan_issue_closed=learn_plan_issue_closed,
        )

        # Get datetime versions for storage (already parsed by YAML)
        last_local_impl_at = header_datetime(plan.header_fields, LAST_LOCAL_IMPL_AT)
        last_remote_impl_at = header_datetime(plan.header_fields, LAST_REMOTE_IMPL_AT)

        # Format time displays
        local_impl = format_relative_time(local_impl_str)
        local_impl_display = local_impl if local_impl else "-"
        remote_impl = format_relative_time(remote_impl_str)
        remote_impl_display = remote_impl if remote_impl else "-"

        # PR info
        pr_number: int | None = None
        pr_url: str | None = None
        pr_title: str | None = None
        pr_state: str | None = None
        pr_head_branch: str | None = None
        pr_display = "-"
        checks_display = "-"

        # Comment counts - "-" when no PR
        resolved_comment_count = 0
        total_comment_count = 0
        comments_display = "-"

        # PR status fields for lifecycle enrichment
        pr_is_draft: bool | None = None
        pr_has_conflicts: bool | None = None
        pr_review_decision: str | None = None
        pr_checks_passing: bool | None = None
        pr_has_unresolved_comments: bool | None = None
        pr_is_stacked: bool | None = None

        if plan_id in pr_linkages:
            issue_prs = pr_linkages[plan_id]
            selected_pr = select_display_pr(issue_prs, exclude_pr_numbers=None)
            if selected_pr is not None:
                pr_number = selected_pr.number
                pr_title = selected_pr.title
                pr_state = selected_pr.state
                pr_head_branch = selected_pr.head_branch
                graphite_url = self._ctx.graphite.get_graphite_url(
                    GitHubRepoId(selected_pr.owner, selected_pr.repo), selected_pr.number
                )
                pr_url = graphite_url if use_graphite and graphite_url else selected_pr.url
                emoji = get_pr_status_emoji(selected_pr)
                if selected_pr.will_close_target:
                    emoji += "🔗"
                pr_display = f"#{selected_pr.number} {emoji}"
                checks_display = format_checks_cell(selected_pr)

                # Extract status fields for lifecycle enrichment
                pr_is_draft = selected_pr.is_draft
                pr_has_conflicts = selected_pr.has_conflicts
                pr_review_decision = selected_pr.review_decision
                pr_checks_passing = selected_pr.checks_passing
                if selected_pr.base_ref_name is not None:
                    pr_is_stacked = selected_pr.base_ref_name not in ("master", "main")

                # Supplement with Graphite's local stack tracking — plan PRs
                # always target master on GitHub even when Graphite-stacked
                if pr_is_stacked is not True and pr_head_branch is not None:
                    parent = self._ctx.branch_manager.get_parent_branch(
                        self._location.root, pr_head_branch
                    )
                    if parent is not None and parent not in ("master", "main"):
                        pr_is_stacked = True

                # Get review thread counts from batched PR data
                if selected_pr.review_thread_counts is not None:
                    resolved_comment_count, total_comment_count = selected_pr.review_thread_counts
                    comments_display = f"{resolved_comment_count}/{total_comment_count}"
                    pr_has_unresolved_comments = total_comment_count > resolved_comment_count
                else:
                    comments_display = "0/0"
                    pr_has_unresolved_comments = False

        # Workflow run info
        run_id: str | None = None
        run_status: str | None = None
        run_conclusion: str | None = None
        run_id_display = "-"
        run_state_display = "-"
        run_url: str | None = None

        if workflow_run is not None:
            run_id = str(workflow_run.run_id)
            run_status = workflow_run.status
            run_conclusion = workflow_run.conclusion
            if plan.url:
                parts = plan.url.split("/")
                if len(parts) >= 5:
                    owner = parts[-4]
                    repo_name = parts[-3]
                    run_url = (
                        f"https://github.com/{owner}/{repo_name}/actions/runs/{workflow_run.run_id}"
                    )
            run_id_display = format_workflow_run_id(workflow_run, run_url)
            run_state_display = format_workflow_outcome(workflow_run)

        # Log entries (empty for now - will be fetched on demand in the modal)
        log_entries: tuple[tuple[str, str, str], ...] = ()

        # Build learn_run_url for pending status
        learn_run_url: str | None = None
        if learn_run_id is not None and plan.url is not None:
            parts = plan.url.split("/")
            if len(parts) >= 5:
                owner = parts[-4]
                repo_name = parts[-3]
                learn_run_url = (
                    f"https://github.com/{owner}/{repo_name}/actions/runs/{learn_run_id}"
                )

        # Format objective display
        objective_url = (
            f"https://github.com/{self._location.repo_id.owner}/{self._location.repo_id.repo}/issues/{objective_issue}"
            if objective_issue is not None
            else None
        )
        objective_display = f"#{objective_issue}" if objective_issue is not None else "-"

        # Compute slug display
        objective_slug_display = "-"
        if plan.body:
            slug = extract_objective_slug(plan.body)
            if slug is not None:
                objective_slug_display = slug[:25]
            elif plan.title:
                stripped = plan.title.removeprefix("Objective: ")
                objective_slug_display = stripped[:25]

        # Parse roadmap for objective-specific fields
        objective_done_nodes = 0
        objective_total_nodes = 0
        objective_progress_display = "-"
        objective_state_display = "-"
        objective_head_state = "-"
        objective_next_node_display = "-"
        objective_head_plans: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
        if plan.body:
            phases, _errors = parse_roadmap(plan.body)
            if phases:
                graph = build_graph(phases)
                summary = compute_graph_summary(graph)
                objective_done_nodes = summary["done"]
                objective_total_nodes = summary["total_nodes"]
                objective_progress_display = f"{objective_done_nodes}/{objective_total_nodes}"
                objective_state_display = build_state_sparkline(graph.nodes)
                next_node = find_graph_next_node(graph, phases)
                if next_node is not None:
                    objective_next_node_display = next_node["id"]
                    node_status = next_node.get("status")
                    min_status = graph.min_dep_status(next_node["id"])
                    if node_status == "planning":
                        objective_head_state = "planning"
                    elif min_status is None or min_status in _TERMINAL_STATUSES:
                        objective_head_state = "ready"
                    else:
                        objective_head_state = min_status.replace("_", " ")

                    # Collect blocking dep PR numbers for the next node
                    target = next((n for n in graph.nodes if n.id == next_node["id"]), None)
                    if target is not None and target.depends_on:
                        node_map = {n.id: n for n in graph.nodes}
